qa_pairs.question: What is a structure in C?
qa_pairs.answer: A structure is a user-defined data type that groups variables of different data types under a single name.

qa_pairs.question: Why are structures used in C?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures are used to store related data of different types together.

qa_pairs.question: What is the syntax for declaring a structure?
qa_pairs.answer: struct StructureName { data_type member1; data_type member2; };

qa_pairs.question: What is a structure member?
qa_pairs.answer: A structure member is a variable declared inside a structure.

qa_pairs.question: How is a structure variable declared?
qa_pairs.answer: By specifying the structure type followed by the variable name.

qa_pairs.question: How are structure members accessed?
qa_pairs.answer: Structure members are accessed using the dot (.) operator.

qa_pairs.question: What is structure initialization?
qa_pairs.answer: Structure initialization is assigning values to structure members at the time of declaration.

qa_pairs.question: Can structures contain different data types?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, structures can contain variables of different data types.

qa_pairs.question: What is a nested structure?
qa_pairs.answer: A nested structure is a structure that contains another structure as a member.

qa_pairs.question: What is an array of structures?
qa_pairs.answer: An array of structures is a collection of multiple structure variables stored in an array.

qa_pairs.question: What is a pointer to a structure?
qa_pairs.answer: A pointer to a structure stores the address of a structure variable.

qa_pairs.question: What is the arrow operator (->)?
qa_pairs.answer: The arrow operator is used to access structure members through a structure pointer.

qa_pairs.question: What are the advantages of structures?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures improve data organization, readability, and management of complex records.

qa_pairs.question: What are the disadvantages of structures?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures may require more memory and can become complex when deeply nested.

qa_pairs.question: What is a union in C?
qa_pairs.answer: A union is a user-defined data type in which all members share the same memory location.

qa_pairs.question: Why are unions used?
qa_pairs.answer: Unions are used to save memory when only one member needs to hold a value at a time.

qa_pairs.question: What is the syntax of a union?
qa_pairs.answer: union UnionName { data_type member1; data_type member2; };

qa_pairs.question: How are union members accessed?
qa_pairs.answer: Union members are accessed using the dot (.) operator.

qa_pairs.question: How is memory allocated in a union?
qa_pairs.answer: Memory equal to the size of the largest member is allocated.

qa_pairs.question: Can all union members hold values simultaneously?
qa_pairs.answer: No, only one member can hold a valid value at a time.

qa_pairs.question: What is the main difference between a structure and a union?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures allocate separate memory for each member, whereas unions share the same memory among all members.

qa_pairs.question: Which uses more memory: structure or union?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures generally use more memory than unions.

qa_pairs.question: Which is more memory efficient: structure or union?
qa_pairs.answer: Unions are more memory efficient because members share memory.

qa_pairs.question: Can structures be passed to functions?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, structures can be passed to functions by value or by reference.

qa_pairs.question: Can unions be passed to functions?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, unions can also be passed to functions.

qa_pairs.question: Where are structures commonly used?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures are used in student records, employee databases, banking systems, and inventory management.

qa_pairs.question: Where are unions commonly used?
qa_pairs.answer: Unions are used in embedded systems, memory optimization, and hardware interfacing.

qa_pairs.question: Why are structures important in programming?
qa_pairs.answer: Structures help represent real-world entities using related data.

qa_pairs.question: Why are unions important in programming?
qa_pairs.answer: Unions help optimize memory usage in resource-constrained applications.

qa_pairs.question: What is the main purpose of structures and unions?
qa_pairs.answer: The main purpose is to organize related data efficiently and improve program design.
